with-playback.description: also seed the playback DBs (up.sh --with-playback). NOTE: up.sh treats --with-playback as a launch directive — without --reset/--restart it implies a full stack bring-up (DO_UP), so on a seed-only invocation this brings the whole stack up rather than seeding in place. Prefer `stack up --with-playback --seed <profile>` when you want the bring-up.
with-playback.name: with-playback

with-qtf-demo.description: also seed the QTF demo data (up.sh --with-qtf-demo)
with-qtf-demo.name: with-qtf-demo

stack:status.description: Show stack health and DB row counts (wraps up.sh --status; read-only).

stack:up.description: Bring the synthetic dev stack up. Real run wraps up.sh; --dry-run prints the closure planner only.

porcelain.description: machine-readable output; no color, minimal noise
porcelain.name: porcelain

output-json.description: emit structured JSON on stdout instead of human-readable text
output-json.name: output-json

dev.description: sibling-repo workspace root (where the saga repos are checked out)
dev.name: dev

state-dir.description: scratch dir for pid files, snapshots, and other run state
state-dir.name: state-dir

soa.description: override path to the soa repo checkout
soa.name: soa

rostering.description: override path to the rostering repo checkout
rostering.name: rostering

program-hub.description: override path to the program-hub repo checkout
program-hub.name: program-hub

saga-dash.description: override path to the saga-dash repo checkout
saga-dash.name: saga-dash

sds.description: override path to the sds (student-data-system) repo checkout
sds.name: sds

qboard.description: override path to the qboard repo checkout
qboard.name: qboard

rtsm.description: override path to the rtsm repo checkout
rtsm.name: rtsm

fleek.description: override path to the fleek repo checkout
fleek.name: fleek

only.description: services to bring up. With --dry-run, a comma-list whose dependency closure is printed. On a real run (M1) only a SINGLE service is accepted (passed through to up.sh); native comma-list + closure bring-up lands at M4.
only.name: only

with-playback.description: include the optional playback services (transcripts, insights, chat)
with-playback.name: with-playback

dry-run.description: plan only: print the resolved closure and exit without touching docker/pnpm
dry-run.name: dry-run

reset.description: truncate + re-seed the data DBs before bringing services up (up.sh --reset)
reset.name: reset

seed.description: seed the named profile after launch (up.sh --seed <profile>). A value is required in the wrapper; up.sh's bare `--seed` default is `roster`, so pass `--seed roster` for that behavior.
seed.name: seed

pull.description: force a full ff-only sync of every sibling repo before build (up.sh --pull)
pull.name: pull

no-auto-pull.description: opt out of the automatic auto-pull pass (up.sh env NO_AUTO_PULL=1)
no-auto-pull.name: no-auto-pull

skip-prep.description: skip the install+build prep pass (up.sh env SKIP_PREP=1)
skip-prep.name: skip-prep

record.description: record session traffic (up.sh --record <mode>). A value is required in the wrapper; up.sh's bare `--record` default is `crdt`, so pass `--record crdt` for that behavior.
record.name: record

tunnel.description: open the public tunnel for the stack (up.sh --tunnel)
tunnel.name: tunnel

login.description: log in the default persona (dev@saga.org) after launch (up.sh --login); use `stack login <email>` to override the persona
login.name: login

sandbox.description: named sandbox to launch into (up.sh --sandbox <name>; accompanies --only)
sandbox.name: sandbox

porcelain.description: machine-readable output; no color, minimal noise
porcelain.name: porcelain

output-json.description: emit structured JSON on stdout instead of human-readable text
output-json.name: output-json

dev.description: sibling-repo workspace root (where the saga repos are checked out)
dev.name: dev

state-dir.description: scratch dir for pid files, snapshots, and other run state
state-dir.name: state-dir

soa.description: override path to the soa repo checkout
soa.name: soa

rostering.description: override path to the rostering repo checkout
rostering.name: rostering

program-hub.description: override path to the program-hub repo checkout
program-hub.name: program-hub

saga-dash.description: override path to the saga-dash repo checkout
saga-dash.name: saga-dash

sds.description: override path to the sds (student-data-system) repo checkout
sds.name: sds

qboard.description: override path to the qboard repo checkout
qboard.name: qboard

rtsm.description: override path to the rtsm repo checkout
rtsm.name: rtsm

fleek.description: override path to the fleek repo checkout
fleek.name: fleek

health-only.description: fast health gate only (verify.sh env VERIFY_HEALTH_ONLY=1)
health-only.name: health-only

tolerate.description: tolerate the named non-fatal failures (M2 — not yet supported)
tolerate.name: tolerate
